articles.title: Tidal to Label AI-Generated Music and Remove Fraudulent Material
articles.description: The company said listeners should know whether the content they are listening to is AI-generated.
articles.content: Music streaming service Tidal says it will start labeling music that's wholly generated by artificial intelligence and will also remove tracks that are fraudulent or deceptive. The company will implement automated tools in mid-July to identify and ta... [1911 chars]

articles.description: AI agents are the latest “next big thing” for AI, but OpenClaw has been working at the idea for a...
articles.content:
AI agents are the latest “next big thing” for AI, but OpenClaw has been working at the idea for a while now. Today, OpenClaw is bringing a gateway app to Android (and iOS) to help bring agents on the go. It also looks… like that.

articles.title: Radar tech AI startup clinches deal for drone attack warning system to Israeli army
articles.description: As part of the deal, Magos will supply hundreds of its radar-based detection systems to protect ground forces against approaching unmanned aerial threats, including fiber-optic drones
articles.content: Israeli startup Magos Systems, a developer of AI-native radar technology, has inked a NIS 21 million ($7 million) agreement to supply defense ground forces with its early-detection alert system against fast-evolving unmanned aerial threats, including... [2811 chars]

articles.title: San Diego’s Qualcomm to buy startup Modular for $3.9 billion in AI push
articles.description: This announcement is one of many from Qualcomm's Investor Day in New York City — each signaling an aggressive push into data centers and AI inference compute.
articles.content:
Qualcomm agreed to acquire AI software startup Modular in an all-stock deal valued at $3.92 billion, the San Diego chipmaker said Wednesday during its Investor Day, as it works to expand beyond smartphone chips into data center and AI markets.

articles.title: AI companionship may reshape how teens learn conflict, boundaries and empathy
articles.description: As teenagers increasingly turn to artificial intelligence chatbots for advice about friendships, family conflicts and romantic ties, researchers are raising concerns that the technology could disrupt how young people learn to navigate human relationships.

articles.title: New AI legislation set to be completed in coming months
articles.description: Thailand aims to finalise a draft of the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Act in this fiscal year, with a focus on balancing the promotion of AI adoption and strict regulation.
articles.content:

articles.title: The Energy Rhythm Model For The AI Workday
articles.description: Time management is an outdated lens for today's workplace. Leaders do not merely need better calendars; they need a better way to design, protect, and renew human energy.
articles.content:
AI gives organizations speed. The leaders who win will be the ones who decide where that newly created capacity goes.
A great jazz band does not create excellence by playing faster every measure.

articles.title: UVic researchers develop training to help people spot AI-generated faces
articles.description: Researchers at the University of Victoria say they have developed a way to help people better identify AI-generated "deepfake" faces, offering a potential tool in the fight against online fraud.

articles.title: I Ditched Duolingo For A Simple Reason
articles.description: Duolingo became less useful when it started using AI to create lessons and added more features behind paywalls, making YouTube lessons a better alternative.
